description: All playable races of Leonoria. Each race group has sub-races with distinct physical profiles, aptitude modifiers, resistances, and traits. Physical data uses randomized ranges for hero generation.
size_order_note: Largest to smallest: Wildmen Ravagers > Wildmen Foresters > Ancients > Ashen Halfbreeds > Common Folk / Step Folk > Swampbrood > Stone Folk > Oakpeople
own_name_note: Own names are what each sub-race calls themselves. Common Folk names are the widely-used outsider labels.

race_groups.name: Ancients
race_groups.general_description: Tall, slender, and nimble with a natural affinity for norium that runs through their blood, granting exceptional magical capacity. Often regarded as attractive and naturally charismatic. 6–7 feet tall with a slender to athletic build — an elegance reflected in skin tint rather than pointed ears. All sub-groups are elitist and arrogant about their own importance, viewing themselves as custodians of wisdom. Their destinies were shaped by The Great Death. They share the same human origin as Common Folk, though this is not widely known.
race_groups.lore: The oldest living memory of Leonoria. No Ancient alive remembers before The Great Death — but all believe their current form was caused by it. Each sub-race interprets this differently: the Aelthiryn as a spiritual gift, the Saereth as a philosophical evolution, the Velkaar as a curse to weaponize, and the Thyraen as something too private to discuss.

sub_races.name: The Secluded
sub_races.description: Forest dwellers and extreme isolationists with an aloof, detached character. Naturally connected to most magical entities. Neutral affinity. Can become passionate about nature-related things. Often mistaken for unfeeling, but they feel deeply — they simply do not share. Among themselves they are called the Aelthiryn.

physical.hero_age_note: Can be generated age 20–95. Physically mature at 30, considered young until 100.
physical.appearance_notes: High, angular cheekbones. Large, luminous eyes (silver, green, gold). Very fine hair. Skin carries a faint green or silver tint — the mark of norium in their blood.

special_traits.name: Forest Bond
special_traits.description: In The Sanctuary Lands or any forested biome, Materium pool regenerates 20% faster. Perception rolls in forested environments gain +10%.

special_traits.name: Aloof Nature
special_traits.description: Presence (Gravitas) rolls in formal social settings suffer -5% — the Aelthiryn find social performance tedious. However, Presence rolls with nature-aligned beings (Oakpeople, wildlife) gain +10%.

special_traits.name: Norium Blood
special_traits.description: The norium in Aelthiryn veins acts as a natural magical conductor. All Materium-school spells cost 10% less Materium Connection.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: The Greys
sub_races.description: City builders, scholars, clerics with a Neutral-Good affinity. Tendencies toward religious fanaticism regarding logic and stoicism. Strong convictions. They see themselves as the rational apex of civilization and find disorder physically uncomfortable. Among themselves they are the Saereth. They prefer the Gleam Havens.

physical.appearance_notes: Pale grey or white skin. Silver or white hair, always styled precisely. Eyes range from pale blue to near-colorless. Angular, clean-cut features.

special_traits.name: Scholar's Discipline
special_traits.description: All Cognition (Erudition) skills level 15% faster. The Saereth absorb knowledge at an exceptional rate.

special_traits.name: Logic Fortress
special_traits.description: Conviction (Resonance / faith-based) rolls gain +10% when acting in alignment with a stated logical principle or oath.

special_traits.name: Broad Conduit
special_traits.description: The Saereth have access to the widest range of Materium schools of any race, though not the deepest. They can access both Light and Shadow schools at reduced potency (50% of normal power for those schools).

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: The Dark Ones
sub_races.description: Secretive, unsocial, and gifted exclusively in dark arts and deceptive fighting. Naturally selfish but with an occasional, surprising compassionate side. Some make great leaders of darker forces — not through love but through terrifying competence. Generally shorter than other Ancients. They call themselves Velkaar and originate in the Forgotten Kingdom.

physical.appearance_notes: Near-black or obsidian skin. Eyes glow a faint red, violet, or pale gold in darkness. Hair is dark, often white in older individuals. Eerily quiet movers — often noticed only when they choose to be.

special_traits.name: Born to the Shadows
special_traits.description: In dark environments (Forgotten Kingdom, night, dungeons) all rolls gain +15%. Shadow connection pool does not drain in darkness.

special_traits.name: Dark Arts Only
special_traits.description: The Velkaar cannot access Light or standard Materium schools at all. They are exclusively attuned to Shadow Arts. All Shadow Arts spells cost 15% less Materium Connection.

special_traits.name: Cold Read
special_traits.description: Once per encounter, can make a Cognition roll to perceive the Conviction level and primary weakness of one enemy.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: The Ice Ancients
sub_races.own_name_etymology: thyr = stillness/cold silence, aen = state of being
sub_races.description: Solitary, mystical, and extremely xenophobic. Their culture and background remain largely unknown to the outside world. They live in The Eternal Winds and leave very rarely. When they do, it is always for an unknown, private reason — and they do not explain themselves. They know themselves as the Thyraen.

physical.appearance_notes: Blue-white or translucent skin. Hair is always white, often with a blue sheen. Eyes are pale ice-blue or near-white. Body temperature is measurably lower than all other races.

special_traits.name: Frozen Blood
special_traits.description: Complete immunity to Climate-Cold damage. In The Eternal Winds, Materium pool regenerates 25% faster. All Chill damage dealt gains +15%.

special_traits.name: Fire Vulnerability
special_traits.description: Fire damage received is increased by 20%. Hot climate damage is also increased by 15%.

special_traits.name: The Vigil
special_traits.description: Thyraen rarely sleep. They enter a waking meditative state. This grants +5% to Perception rolls — they are always partially aware.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

race_groups.name: Common Folk
race_groups.general_description: Ordinary Caucasian to Mediterranean humans, average in build — from small to large examples. The original humans from before The Great Death. They live ordinary human lifespans of around 70–100 years and are the most plentiful race on Leonoria. They rarely show magical affinity — if they do, it is exclusively in Lightwielding magics (Clerics and Templars) and in some rare cases Dark arts (Shamans of Step Folk and Northerners). No unified history or mythology.
race_groups.lore: Every scholar who has tried to trace Common Folk history before The Great Death has failed. The records simply don't exist — which some argue means they were there, and that The Great Death destroyed everything. Others say they appeared after. The Common Folk themselves don't particularly care. They are busy living.

sub_races.name: Midlander
sub_races.description: The classic commoner of Leonoria. Highly varied in appearance — no unifying physical trait. Skilled traders, leaders, bards, and diplomats. Prone to religious fanaticism. They built the Midlands into the breadbasket of the continent through sheer stubbornness and organizational skill. They call themselves the Erdan. Strong presence in The Gleam Havens as well.

physical.appearance_notes: The most variable appearance of any sub-race. Every skin tone, hair color, and build exists among the Erdan. No unifying physical trait.

special_traits.name: Generalist
special_traits.description: No class is restricted or penalized for an Erdan. All classes are available at no modifier cost.

special_traits.name: Corruption Prone
special_traits.description: Midlanders are easily corrupted by long exposure to Shadow Arts or extreme moral failures. Conviction (Resonance) suffers -5% when performing morally grey actions repeatedly.

special_traits.name: Diplomatic Touch
special_traits.description: +5% to all Presence (Eloquence) rolls in trade and negotiation contexts.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: Northerner
sub_races.description: Isolationist hunters, traders, and fierce warriors. Heavier build with weathered or ruddy skin and mostly dark or auburn hair. Strong convictions around their faith. Stoic, practical, and deeply suspicious of outsiders. Known for exceptional endurance in hostile conditions. They call themselves the Skarn. They dwell in northern parts of the Midlands, deeper forests, the Dark Forests, and reach as far as the Eternal Winds.

physical.appearance_notes: Heavier build than other Common Folk. Skin often weathered and ruddy. Hair predominantly dark or auburn. Eyes typically grey or blue. Dress practically and rarely for show.

special_traits.name: Cold Endurance
special_traits.description: Skarn do not suffer Conviction penalties from Climate-Cold environments. They are adapted.

special_traits.name: Forest Instinct
special_traits.description: +8% to Survival, Tracking, and Navigation skill rolls in forested or boreal environments.

special_traits.name: Faith Hold
special_traits.description: When following a faith-based religion, Conviction (Resonance) gains an additional +5%. Their faith is unusually strong and focused.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: Step Folk
sub_races.description: Dark-skinned, hardy, rugged people shaped by open plains and a harder travel life. Their home is the Outer Steppes, also the Badlands and plains of The Midlands, with some tribes in the outskirts of the Dark Forests. Skilled all-around warriors and traders. Neither exceptional warriors nor exceptional mages, but rarely outmatched in pure versatility. They call themselves the Zai'Keth.

physical.appearance_notes: Dark-skinned, lean and wiry builds optimized for travel. Hair is often kept practical — braided, short, or covered. Tribal markings or tattoos are common in Zai'Keth culture.

special_traits.name: Nomadic Adaptability
special_traits.description: +8% to Metabolism (Stamina) rolls on the world map. Zai'Keth tire more slowly than others on long marches.

special_traits.name: Trade Tongue
special_traits.description: +10% to all Presence (Eloquence) rolls in marketplace or trade contexts. The Zai'Keth have an instinctive read for value.

special_traits.name: Heat Adaptation
special_traits.description: Do not suffer Conviction penalties from Climate-Hot environments. Adapted to the Outer Steppes and Badlands.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

race_groups.name: Wildmen
race_groups.general_description: Rugged, hairy, muscular barbarian-like humanoids with some bear-like features. Not plentiful but deeply feared. Partially physically afflicted and maddened by The Great Death. Brutish, tall, and extraordinarily strong — up to 8 feet tall. All are resistant to cold weather and have strong natural resistance to moral afflictions. Foresters have an affinity for communing with wildlife magic. Ravagers are fearsome warriors with two-handed weapons and shamanism in their culture.
race_groups.lore: The Wildmen were changed by The Great Death in a different way than the Ancients. Where the Ancients were refined and elevated, the Wildmen were enlarged and partially maddened. Their connection to the pre-Death world is visceral and physical — something in their blood remembers violence, and they are never quite free of it.

sub_races.name: Foresters
sub_races.description: Secluded, hairy, mossy, camouflaged, rough and beast-like in appearance. 6–7 feet tall. Peaceful until threatened. Extreme isolationists with shamanistic faiths. Naturally neutral in alignment — they are not cruel by nature, just absolutely self-reliant. They know themselves as the Gruthen. Home is the Dark Forests, with settlements in nearby forested biomes; rumors of an isolated tribe as far south as The Boglands.

physical.appearance_notes: Heavy, moss-like hair covering much of the body. Green-brown skin tones. Bark-like texture to forearms and shoulders. Eyes are usually amber or dark brown. Move surprisingly quietly for their size despite being 6–7 feet tall.

special_traits.name: Natural Camouflage
special_traits.description: In forested or boggy environments, Stealth skill is treated as 15 levels higher. Animals do not become hostile to a Gruthen unprovoked.

special_traits.name: Shamanistic Healing
special_traits.description: Can use Bestial Communion to accelerate natural HP regeneration by 10% between battles. Requires no Materium pool.

special_traits.name: Great Stature
special_traits.description: All heavy weapons (2-handed) have their minimum Vigor requirement reduced by 10 for Gruthen. They carry as if one size category larger.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: Ravagers
sub_races.description: Very large, brutal-looking, muscular warrior types with a savage presence. 7–8 feet tall. Aggressive. Known for cannibalism. Large frontline mercenaries and destroyers. Naturally dark in alignment — though on rare occasions, benign neutral outliers appear, often expelled from their tribes. They call themselves the Krothan. Home is in the Dark Forests.

physical.appearance_notes: Enormous, heavily muscled. Scarred and war-marked skin. Skin tones from dark grey to deep red-brown. Eyes are small and predatory. Teeth are unusually pronounced. The smell of smoke and iron follows them.

special_traits.name: Unstoppable
special_traits.description: Krothan cannot be knocked back, rooted, or staggered by any physical force (Blunt stagger immune). They continue moving through terrain that would stop others.

special_traits.name: Cannibalistic Sustenance
special_traits.description: After defeating a living enemy, a Krothan can choose to consume them. This restores 15% of max HP. Has a Conviction cost (-3 Resonance) if team members with Conviction above 60 observe this.
special_traits.alignment_note: Non-dark Krothan outliers can suppress this ability but cannot access the HP restoration.

special_traits.name: Fear Presence
special_traits.description: Enemies with Conviction below 40 must pass a Conviction (Tenacity) check on first encountering a Krothan or lose 1 initiative turn.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

race_groups.name: The Elementally Afflicted
race_groups.general_description: Various humanoids with scarred or mutated skin due to environment and adaptation. Mutants whose destinies were shaped at The Great Death when the laws of nature were rewritten. Where the Ancients were elevated spiritually, the Afflicted were fused with the physical world itself — wood, stone, swamp, or void.
race_groups.lore: Some scholars argue the Elementally Afflicted are not one group but several unrelated accidents of The Great Death. What they share is an elemental bond to some part of the natural or subnatural world.

sub_races.name: Oakpeople
sub_races.own_name_etymology: tal = root/trunk/that which stands, arin = people/kin
sub_races.description: Small, hobbit-like people with bark and leaf-like growths and a nature-bound look. Extremely isolationist and aloof. Have their own nature-friendly faiths unknown to outsiders. Those who wander the wider world often do so because they were set out as children in the forest, with no cultural connection to their origin. Gifted in lockpicking, thievery, and small weapons; agile and nimble. Strong resilience against all forms of magic; good insights in athropium and earthern magics; some Dark Illusion skills. They know themselves as the Talarin. Originally reside in The Sanctuary.

physical.appearance_notes: Covered in real living leaf and bark growths. Skin ranges from bark-brown to deep green. Hair grows as a tangle of small branches and leaves. Eyes are luminous green or gold. Small and deceptively light on their feet.

special_traits.name: Nature's Apex
special_traits.description: Talarin deal 20% more Athropium damage than any other race. Animals are always neutral toward them regardless of circumstance.

special_traits.name: Communication Struggle
special_traits.description: All Presence (Eloquence) and social skill rolls suffer -15%. This represents genuine difficulty processing non-natural language and social norms.

special_traits.name: Living Armor
special_traits.description: Leaf and bark growths provide innate equivalent of light armor. Cannot be disarmed of this protection. Restores overnight in natural environments.

special_traits.name: Fire Sensitive
special_traits.description: Fire damage received is increased by 10%. They instinctively retreat from open flames.

special_traits.name: Nimble Fingers
special_traits.description: Talarin gain +15% to all Lockpicking, Pickpocket, and Sleight of Hand rolls. Their small, dexterous hands are naturally suited for fine manipulation.

naming_rules.surname_rule: The surname is always the settlement of birth, taken from data/map/oakspeople_settlements.json. It is not a family name — it changes if the individual permanently relocates and puts down roots elsewhere.
examples: Zorion of Heart Of The Oak

sub_races.name: Stone Folk
sub_races.description: Short, sturdy, rock-like people tied to mountain living. Their shiny, reflecting silvery eyes are adapted for darkness. They strongly prefer their own race and are deeply suspicious of outsiders. Gifted melee warriors, especially with maces and axes. All have an affinity for Earthern magic, with some being masters of it. Incredibly tough with huge resistances to all forms of damage except poisons. They call themselves the Dornak. Home is the eastern mountains of The Sanctuary, with mountain settlements across the continent and a presence in The Forgotten Kingdom.

physical.appearance_notes: Rock-grey, slate, or iron-colored skin. Very dense musculature. Shiny, reflecting silvery eyes adapted for darkness. Hair grows thick and often contains trace mineral deposits (flecks of quartz or iron visible). Some Dornak have partial skin calcification.

special_traits.name: Stone Skin
special_traits.description: Physical Hardening starts 10 levels higher than other races. Heavy armor proficiency comes naturally — no class restriction needed.

special_traits.name: Darkblood Resilience
special_traits.description: Dornak have an unusual mineral composition in their blood that strongly resists Darkblood manipulation. Darkblood status effects have 50% reduced duration.

special_traits.name: Underground Navigation
special_traits.description: In The Forgotten Kingdom or any underground environment, all rolls gain +10%. Dornak have a near-perfect sense of direction underground.

special_traits.name: Slow but Certain
special_traits.description: -5 to Agility starting value. Dornak are not nimble, but they are almost impossible to knock from their feet.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: Swampbrood
sub_races.own_name_etymology: ur = deep/below/submerged, veth = those who endure/live within
sub_races.description: Human-sized, swamp-adapted, damp, murky, and mutated in appearance with black eyes. All Urveth naturally infuse every attack with foul venom — slowing the target's attack initiative by 5% and causing 1 HP bleed per round until rested. Skilled in shadow illusions and blow-dart-type weapons; crafty hunters who hate cities. Very skilled in Blight magic and poison-infusion potions. Their culture revolves around fermentation, decay, and the cycles of rot. They call themselves the Urveth and home is The Boglands.

physical.appearance_notes: Hunched posture from a crooked spine. Skin has a mottled, wet look — greenish, grey, or mud-brown. Fingers are elongated with slight webbing. Eyes are very large, pure black, and adapted to low light. Carry a persistent earthy, fermented smell.

special_traits.name: Venom Infusion (Passive)
special_traits.description: Every Urveth attack — melee or ranged — naturally carries foul venom. Each hit slows the target's attack initiative by 5% and causes 1 HP bleed per round until the target rests or is treated. This is a passive racial trait requiring no Materium.

special_traits.name: Bog Immunity
special_traits.description: Completely immune to all Venomous and Poisonous effects. Blight damage taken is reduced by 50%.

special_traits.name: Swamp Walker
special_traits.description: No movement penalty in swamp, bog, or wetland terrain. All other races suffer standard movement penalties in these tiles.

special_traits.name: Fermentation Arts
special_traits.description: +15% to Alchemy (Potion Brewing) skill rolls. Urveth alchemists are renowned for their biological and rot-based compounds.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.

sub_races.name: The Ashen Halfbreeds
sub_races.own_name_etymology: Ul = self/people, kar = ash/cinder, ash = state of becoming
sub_races.own_name_meaning: We who became ash and lived
sub_races.description: Tall, physically corrupted former Dark One Ancients with an ashen, twisted look — no hair, deeply scarred and crusted skin, but hauntingly beautiful glowing red or orange eyes. Exiled from the Forgotten Kingdom, they were left to suffer and change in the isolated central parts of The Badlands — a transformative, mutating biome. Skilled general warriors, gifted in Earthern, Blight, and Fire magic. Very resilient to all forms of fire damage and venoms. They call themselves the Ul'Karash.

physical.appearance_notes: Ash-grey or white skin with dark crack-like markings tracing their veins. No hair whatsoever — the exile burned it away permanently. Eyes glow a haunting red or orange. Movement is fluid but slightly wrong — like watching something move that should not be alive.

special_traits.name: Ash-Born Resilience
special_traits.description: Fire damage received is reduced by 25%. All venom and poison effects have 50% reduced duration on Ul'Karash. The Badlands' transformative environment hardened them against extremes.

special_traits.name: Purelight Vulnerability
special_traits.description: Purelight damage is increased by 15% against Ul'Karash. Their corrupted nature reacts violently to divine light.

special_traits.name: Void Erasure
special_traits.description: Once per battle, can use Voidreaving to partially erase one enemy debuff from the battlefield — removing it from up to 2 enemies in range.

special_traits.name: Badlands Mutation
special_traits.description: Ul'Karash roll all physical resistance checks with a +5% baseline bonus against damage from environmental sources (heat, acid, radiation). The Badlands changed what they are at a fundamental level.

naming_rules._placeholder: To be filled in — first_names list, surname rules, format, and examples.
